title: Nephelium informe
uses: Nephelium lappaceum is often confused with Nephelium informe.
benefits: Nephelium lappaceum, also known as the Rambutan tree, is the source of the Nephelium lappaceum fruit, which is informally known as Nephelium informe.  Nephelium informe has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian cultures for its potential health benefits. It is rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that may help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. The fruit has been associated with supporting cardiovascular health, improving immune function, and providing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. However, more research is needed to confirm the specific health benefits of Nephelium informe.
properties: Nephelium lappaceum, also known as the rambutan, is a plant species with several therapeutic properties. The fruit and other parts of the rambutan plant have been used in traditional medicine for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The rambutan has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and skin problems. The plant's extracts have also been found to have potential in the treatment of various diseases, such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. Further research is needed to confirm the full extent of the rambutan's therapeutic properties.
constituents: Nephelium lappaceum, also known as the Rambutan fruit, contains various active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. These compounds have been reported to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The fruit also contains a significant amount of vitamin C and minerals like iron, potassium, and magnesium. Additionally, Nephelium lappaceum has been found to contain certain alkaloids and saponins, which may contribute to its medicinal properties. The specific combination and concentration of these active constituents can vary depending on factors like the fruit's ripeness and growing conditions.
preparations: Nephelium lappaceum, the plant commonly known as the rambutan, has various herbal preparations, primarily in traditional medicine. The fruits, seeds, and leaves of the plant are used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and extracts. In some cultures, the rambutan fruit is used to treat fever, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues. The plant's extracts are also used to treat various skin conditions due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand the efficacy and potential side effects of Nephelium lappaceum herbal preparations.
side_effects: Nephelium lappaceum, also known as the rambutan, has been reported to cause side effects such as gastrointestinal issues in some individuals. These issues may include nausea and stomach discomfort after consuming rambutan. Consuming large quantities of rambutan may also lead to allergic reactions in sensitive individuals. In rare cases, eating rambutan seeds has been associated with the release of a compound called amygdalin, which can release cyanide. However, it is essential to note that these side effects are generally rare and more research is needed to confirm the severity of these reactions.
